title: 10 best herbal teas for oily skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily skin are infusions made from plants that are particularly beneficial for reducing excess oil and improving the overall health of the skin. These teas contain various compounds, such as tannins, flavonoids, and antioxidants, that help regulate sebum production, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritation. Examples of herbal teas that are helpful for oily skin include green tea, chamomile, nettle, and yarrow. By incorporating these teas into their skincare routine, individuals can experience a decrease in shine, fewer breakouts, and an overall improvement in the appearance and texture of their skin. This can lead to increased confidence, a more balanced complexion, and a healthier relationship with one's skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production. The tea's antioxidants can also protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily skin. Additionally, lemon balm tea has antibacterial properties that can help prevent acne caused by excess oil and bacteria. Drinking lemon balm tea or using it as a toner can help soothe irritation and reduce the appearance of pores, leading to a more balanced and clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea has antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria that can contribute to acne and other skin issues associated with oily skin.
Astringent properties: Lemon balm tea contains tannins that have astringent properties, which can help to reduce the production of oil in the skin.
Calming properties: Lemon balm tea has calming properties that can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to oily skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have astringent properties, making them ideal for controlling excess oil on the skin.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are rich in essential oils, which can help regulate sebum production and improve the overall texture of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 2 cups of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves to the boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the lemon balm leaves.
Drink the tea while it's warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and cleanse the skin. The menthol in peppermint tea can help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. Additionally, peppermint tea has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and give the skin a matte finish. Drinking peppermint tea can also help detoxify the body, which can lead to clearer, healthier-looking skin. Regularly consuming peppermint tea can be a simple and natural way to manage oily skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Peppermint tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent acne and other skin infections caused by bacteria that thrive on oily skin.
Astringent: Peppermint tea contains tannins that act as an astringent, helping to reduce the size of pores and control oil production.
Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritation and reduce redness associated with oily skin.
Antioxidant: Peppermint tea is rich in antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the signs of aging.
Balancing: Peppermint tea helps balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing excess oil and leaving the skin looking matte and clear.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are rich in menthol and menthone, which help to control sebum production and reduce oily skin.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin.

Seeds: Peppermint seeds are a good source of essential oils, including menthol and menthone, which have astringent properties that can help regulate oil production and improve the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, leaving only the liquid.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the tea, or use it as a toner by soaking a cotton ball and applying it to oily skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with oily skin because it contains antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight acne-causing bacteria. Thyme is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Moreover, thyme tea has astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. By drinking thyme tea or using it as a toner, you can help keep your skin healthy and balanced.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tea's antimicrobial property helps control oily skin by reducing the growth of acne-causing bacteria. This property is important for oily skin as it helps prevent acne breakouts.
Astringent: Thyme tea has astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce the production of sebum, leading to less oily skin.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory property soothes irritated skin and reduces redness, providing relief for inflamed oily skin.
Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier skin and reducing the signs of aging in oily skin.
Exfoliating: Thyme tea's exfoliating property helps remove dead skin cells and unclog pores, leading to a more balanced and clear complexion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have antibacterial properties, making them effective in controlling excess oil and preventing acne.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils that help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin.
Stems: Thyme stems have astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce the shine associated with oily skin.
Rhyzomes: Thyme rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin and reduce redness caused by excessive oil production.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Steep thyme leaves in boiled water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove thyme leaves.
Drink the thyme tea, or let it cool and use as a toner for oily skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with oily skin because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. The tannins in sage tea have an antiperspirant effect, which helps control excess oil on the skin. Additionally, sage tea is rich in antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy and clear skin. Drinking sage tea or using it as a toner can help balance oil production and improve the overall appearance of oily skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Sage tea has antiseptic properties that help kill bacteria and prevent acne, which is important for oily skin as it can help reduce inflammation and prevent breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, volatile oils, and flavonoids.

Astringent: Sage tea has astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce the production of sebum, which is important for oily skin as it can help regulate oil production and improve the overall appearance of the skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, which is important for oily skin as it can help soothe irritation and reduce the appearance of inflamed pores. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Sage tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for oily skin as it can help prevent premature aging and improve the overall health of the skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Exfoliating: Sage tea has exfoliating properties that help remove dead skin cells and unclog pores, which is important for oily skin as it can help improve the texture and tone of the skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include alpha-hydroxy acids and beta-hydroxy acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage leaves help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
Flowers: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, sage flowers can help balance oiliness and reduce the appearance of large pores.
Seeds: High in polyunsaturated fatty acids and vitamins, sage seeds can help nourish and strengthen the skin, reducing excess oil production.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the sage leaves in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the sage tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Allow the sage tea to cool to a drinkable temperature before consuming.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help regulate sebum production. Rosemary is also rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The tea can be used as a toner or applied directly to the skin to help reduce shine and improve the overall appearance of oily skin. Additionally, rosemary tea has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and give the skin a smoother, more refined appearance. Drinking rosemary tea can also provide internal benefits, as it can help detoxify the body and promote healthy skin from within.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and soothe irritated oily skin, thanks to its active constituents, including rosmarinic acid and camphor.
Antiseptic: Its antiseptic property aids in preventing acne and bacterial infections, which is crucial for maintaining clear oily skin, due to the presence of compounds like eucalyptol and α-pinene.
Astringent: Rosemary tea's astringent property helps control excess oil production and tighten pores, contributing to a matte finish on oily skin, attributable to tannins and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property protects oily skin from free radical damage and premature aging, making rosemary tea an important addition to skincare routines, owing to rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Rosemary tea promotes blood circulation, aiding in the removal of toxins and improving skin health, which is beneficial for oily skin as it encourages cell turnover and regeneration, attributed to its camphene and caffeic acid content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain active compounds that help regulate sebum production, reducing oily skin.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin and help control excess oil.
Stems: Contain essential oils that help balance skin's natural oil production and promote skin health.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, seeds help nourish and protect oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, boil 2 cups of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the rosemary leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature before consuming.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tea helps with oily skin because it contains antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production. The tannins present in marjoram tea have astringent qualities that can help tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oily skin. Additionally, marjoram tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy and clear skin. Drinking marjoram tea or using it as a toner can help reduce excess oil and improve the overall health and appearance of oily skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Marjoram tea has antiseptic properties that help prevent acne and other skin infections caused by bacteria, which is important for oily skin as it is more prone to breakouts. The active constituents responsible for its antiseptic properties are primarily its essential oils, including terpinen-4-ol and γ-terpinene.

Astringent: Marjoram tea acts as an astringent, helping to tighten pores and reduce the production of sebum, which is beneficial for oily skin as it helps control excessive oiliness and shine. The tannins present in marjoram tea give it its astringent properties.

Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritation and reduce redness, which is important for oily skin as it can help alleviate inflammation and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin.

Antioxidant: Marjoram tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for oily skin as it can help prevent premature aging and promote a healthier complexion. The active constituents responsible for its antioxidant properties include rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Marjoram tea helps improve blood circulation, which is important for oily skin as it promotes the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the skin cells, helping to keep the skin healthy and promoting a more even skin tone. The active constituents responsible for its circulation-boosting properties include its essential oils and various flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the marjoram tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing excess oil production and soothing irritated skin.
Flowers: The flowers of the marjoram tea plant contain essential oils that help regulate sebum production, promoting a balanced skin tone and reducing the appearance of oily skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the marjoram tea plant are a natural source of antioxidants and have astringent properties, making them effective in tightening pores and reducing excess oil on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried marjoram herb.
Steep - Allow the marjoram to steep in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain - Strain the marjoram leaves from the tea, discarding the leaves.
Cool - Let the marjoram tea cool to a drinkable temperature.
Serve - Drink the marjoram tea, ideally before meals, to help manage oily skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. The tea is also rich in antioxidants that help to combat free radicals, which can cause skin damage. Additionally, German chamomile tea contains azulene, a natural compound that has been shown to regulate sebum production, helping to reduce excess oil on the skin. By using German chamomile tea as a toner or compress, you can help to balance your skin's natural oil production and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea helps reduce inflammation associated with oily skin, soothing irritations and reducing redness (active constituents
Astringent: It helps to tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin (active constituent
Antibacterial: German chamomile tea inhibits bacterial growth, preventing acne and skin infections (active constituents
Sebum-regulating: It helps control sebum production, reducing excess oil on the skin (active constituents
Soothing: German chamomile tea has a calming effect on the skin, alleviating irritation and providing relief for oily skin (active constituents
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for oily skin due to their anti-inflammatory and oil-reducing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile tea plant are used in making medicinal tea for oily skin because they contain flavonoids that help regulate sebum production.
Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for oily skin as they are rich in essential fatty acids that help soothe and moisturize the skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Steep the chamomile in hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the chamomile flowers from the tea.
Drink the chamomile tea warm, 2-3 times a day for oily skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with oily skin because it has natural detoxifying properties that can help eliminate toxins and impurities from the body. This tea is rich in antioxidants and vitamins, including A, C, and E, which can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. Dandelion tea also has diuretic properties that can help flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body, reducing the appearance of oily skin. Additionally, dandelion tea is known to improve digestion and liver function, which can help eliminate toxins from the body and promote healthy skin. By promoting overall health and well-being, dandelion tea can help reduce the symptoms of oily skin and promote a clearer, more radiant complexion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers help regulate sebum production and soothe inflammation in oily skin.
Leaves: Containing high amounts of vitamins and minerals, dandelion leaves help balance skin oil levels and reduce skin irritation.
Roots: Known for their detoxifying properties, dandelion roots aid in eliminating toxins and controlling excess oil in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of dandelion flowers from an organic, pesticide-free source.
Rinse the flowers under cold water, then let them air dry for 5 minutes.
Steep the dried dandelion flowers in 16 ounces of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the flowers, then let the tea cool.
Drink the cooled dandelion tea twice a day for best results.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tea can help reduce inflammation, which is important for oily skin as it can alleviate skin irritation and reduce the appearance of acne. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds.

Detoxification: Dandelion tea promotes liver function and aids in toxin elimination, which is crucial for oily skin as it helps regulate sebum production and improve skin health. The active constituents responsible for this effect are taraxacin, taraxacerin, and taraxacoside.

Antibacterial: Dandelion tea has antibacterial properties that can help prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria, making it beneficial for oily skin. The antibacterial activity is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for oily skin as it can reduce the signs of aging and improve overall skin health. The antioxidant properties come from various flavonoids and carotenoids present in dandelion tea.

Sebum regulation: Dandelion tea can help regulate sebum production, which is important for oily skin as it can reduce excessive oiliness and improve skin texture. This is due to the presence of phytosterols, which have been shown to have a sebum-regulating effect.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce excess oil production and soothe irritated skin. The tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which can help regulate sebum production and improve skin tone. Additionally, pot marigold tea has mild astringent properties that can help tighten pores and reduce the appearance of shine. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a toner can help maintain the skin's natural balance and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation associated with oily skin, soothing irritations and reducing redness. This property is important for oily skin because it alleviates skin issues like acne and irritation caused by excess sebum. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and carotenoids.

Astringent: Pot marigold tea has astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin. This property is important for oily skin because it helps minimize the shine and greasiness caused by excessive oil production. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins.

Antibacterial: Pot marigold tea has antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria. This property is important for oily skin because it helps prevent and treat acne, which is often a concern for people with oily skin. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for oily skin because it helps maintain the skin's health and appearance, reducing the signs of aging. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and carotenoids.

Sebum-regulating: Pot marigold tea has sebum-regulating properties that help balance the production of sebum. This property is important for oily skin because it helps maintain the skin's natural oil balance, reducing the appearance of oily skin. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, the flowers help regulate sebum production in the skin.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help control excess oil and soothe irritated skin.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, the seeds aid in balancing sebum levels and maintaining skin's natural moisture.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the flowers.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production. Its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and soothe irritated skin, while its astringent properties can help tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin. Yarrow tea is also rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By drinking yarrow tea or using it as a toner, you can help balance your skin's natural oil production and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tea has astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce the production of sebum, making it ideal for oily skin. This property is due to the presence of tannins in yarrow.

Antibacterial: Yarrow tea has antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria that can cause acne and other skin infections, making it an effective treatment for oily skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and terpenoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as azulene and camphor.

Sebum-regulating: Yarrow tea has sebum-regulating properties that help to balance the production of sebum, reducing the appearance of oily skin. This property is due to the presence of lactones such as thujone and chamazulene.

Tonic: Yarrow tea has tonic properties that help to improve the overall health and appearance of the skin. This property is due to the presence of various nutrients and antioxidants such as vitamin C, vitamin E, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of yarrow tea plant are rich in anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them effective in reducing excess oil production in the skin.
Flowers: The flowers of yarrow tea plant contain azulene, a compound known for its sebum-regulating properties, making them ideal for managing oily skin.
Seeds: The seeds of yarrow tea plant are a potent source of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which can help balance and nourish oily skin.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried yarrow herb.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture to remove yarrow herb pieces, then let the liquid cool.
Add 1 teaspoon of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of honey to the yarrow tea.
Stir the tea until the honey is fully dissolved, then drink it while warm.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily skin with herbal teas is by using them as toners. Brew strongly aromatic herbal teas like chamomile, green, or black tea, then let them cool. These teas contain tannins that help remove excess oil and reduce shine. After cleansing, apply the cooled tea to your face using a cotton ball. The astringent properties of these teas will help tighten pores and give your skin a matte finish. Regular use will help regulate oil production, leaving your skin looking fresher and healthier.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal teas include acne and skin inflammation. For acne, herbal teas like chamomile, spearmint, and green tea are often recommended due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These teas can help reduce redness, swelling, and the number of acne-causing bacteria on the skin. For skin inflammation, herbal teas such as calendula and echinacea can be beneficial. These teas have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and heal irritated skin. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can be helpful, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe skin conditions.
